name: Chocolate Cointreau Truffles
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the 9 oz. chocolate and heavy cream in a saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally until the chocolate is melted.
2. Remove from heat and pour into a mixing bowl.
3. Stir in the Cointreau or other liqueur.
4. Place in the refrigerator for approximately 2 hours or freeze for approximately 1/2 hour or until set (soft when pressed with finger).
5. Cover a pastry sheet with a piece of waxed paper.
6. Scoop out mounds of 1 1/2 - 2 teaspoons, depending on the desired size using two spoons or a pastry bag fitted with a No. 6 plain tube held about 1/4 inch above the surface of the paper.
7. Place in the refrigerator until firm, at least 4 hours but preferably overnight.
8. To make the chocolate covering, place the 1 lb. of chocolate in a saucepan and put in the oven set to the lowest possible temperature (electric stove) or on pilot (gas) for approximately 20 - 30 minutes. For a quicker method, bring water to boil in a bain-marie or double boiler, remove from heat, and place the pan containing chocolate into hot water. Stir frequently until melted.
9. Allow to cool to body temperature when no difference is noticed when touching the chocolate with the tip of the smallest finger.
10. Spread cocoa powder evenly on a flat pan or plate.
11. Remove truffles from the refrigerator and drop 2-4 at a time into the coating chocolate using two forks.
12. Turn the truffles to coat well, lift with one fork, tapping the other fork to eliminate excess chocolate, then drop onto the pan containing cocoa powder.
13. Roll and coat each truffle with the powder, then push aside.
14. When firm, transfer to a bowl and refrigerate or freeze until ready to serve.
15. Yield: 60-70 pieces (1 1/2 lbs).
